calendar.matters:
NOTICE OF HEARING AND TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER (ELDER OR DEPENDENT ADULT ABUSE)
Off Calendar Feb-10-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Elder Abuse/Dependent Adult TRO hearing this day and was called with case number PED-10-293116 (Bill Tang aka Chiu Yee Tang v. Cheuklun Tang aka James Tang).

Dept. 204, Judge: Mary Wiss, Court Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR# 11312, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Bailiff: Deputy Capozolli.

Heidi Li of the Asian Pacific Islander Legal Outreach, for the Petitioner: Tracy Tang, present.

Respondent: Cheuklun Tang in pro per, present.

The parties were sworn.

The Court hears testimony, receives evidence and after hearing the evidence presented, the Petitioner's order is Granted as follows: Respondent shall stay at least 100 yards away from the protected person: Tracy Tang aka Chui Yiu Tang and her residence.

Respondent shall not Harass, Attack, Strike, Threaten, Assault (sexually or otherwise), Hit, Follow, Stalk, Destroy Personal Property, Keep under Surveillance, or Block Movements.

Respondent is to immediately move out from and not return to 319 Byxbee Street, San Francisco, CA 94112

Respondent cannot own, possess, have, buy or try to buy, receive or try to receive, or in any other way get a gun or firearm and must turn in or sell guns or firearms.

Expires February 10, 2013

Order signed in open court.

NOTICE OF HEARING AND TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER (ELDER OR DEPENDENT ADULT ABUSE)
Off Calendar Jan-27-2010 Reset To Feb-10-10 At 11:00 A.M. In Dept 204 For Order To Show Cause
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Elder Abuse/Dependent Adult TRO hearing this day.

Dept. 204, Commissioner: Sue Kaplan, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Bailiff: Deputy Capozolli.

Heidi Li of the Asian Pacific Islander Legal Outreach, for the Petitioner: Tracy Tang, present.

Respondent: Cheuklun Tang in pro per, present.

Off the record: there being no stipulation by the Respondent to the Commissioner the matter was continued to February 10, 2010 to be heard before Judge Mary Wiss.

A reissuance was granted to February 10, 2010 (order signed).
